response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum bar on paper — roughly 19 years of hands-on software experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, plus a Bachelor's in Computer Science — but the profile is that of a strong senior/staff builder, not a director-level AI strategist. Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling is promising but shallow (about a year), and there is no evidence of recognized AI expertise, shipped AI products or platforms, engineering leadership at scale, or advising strategic customers and partners, which are the core of this role.
strengths: Nearly two decades of broad, polyglot engineering experience (web, mobile, embedded, low-latency backend)
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of an open-source geo-data platform used in production by NGOs
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing through conference talks, workshops, and courses
concerns: No principal/director-level scope: career has stayed at senior/staff IC on product feature work
AI experience is recent (~1 year of agent tooling/MCP work) with no recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products
Limited cloud platform strategy depth (some AWS CDK and Kubernetes usage, not platform-level design or advisory work)

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. director-level IC role
Lack of recognized AI expertise or AI products
Meets minimum experience and degree requirements with strong builder fluency
